title: Recruiter, International Private Bank, Vice President
description:
This is a great opportunity to leverage your ability to build strong relationships with managers and candidates to deliver hiring goals.

Human Resources (HR) plays a critical role in driving the employee experience, shaping the firm’s culture, and building a diverse and inclusive workforce. We are a strategic partner to the business—working with leaders across the firm to hire, develop, and retain the best people, and help meet business goals.

As a Recruiter for International Private Bank (IPB) at the Vice President level, you will support end-to-end recruitment efforts across our APAC IPB business. In this role, you will assist with all aspects of Talent Acquisition and Talent Management for APAC IPB, including competitive intelligence, market mapping, and confidential project work.

Job Responsibilities

• Support full lifecycle recruitment, including sourcing, screening, scheduling, and managing candidates through to completion

• Assist in managing a client group or multiple client groups within IPB

• Source qualified candidates through various channels and support the recruitment process

• Develop an understanding of the line of business recruiting needs by participating in client meetings and staying aware of changes within the business

• Provide recruiting updates and support to business group heads as required

• Ensure a positive candidate experience with timely communication and feedback throughout the recruitment process

• Educate candidates on career growth potential, benefits, and compensation philosophy, promoting both the firm and the opportunity

• Support compensation conversations with hiring managers and candidates

• Build rapport and partner with stakeholders for candidate development and offers

• Uphold the firm's focus on diversity

Required Qualifications, Capabilities, and Skills

• Minimum 12 years of recruitment experience, ideally within private banking or financial services

• Bachelors degree or equivalent

• Experience with full lifecycle recruiting in a fast-paced corporate environment or search firm

• Strong organizational and project management skills with the ability to manage multiple assignments

• Good judgment and problem-solving skills

• Effective interpersonal and communication skills; comfortable interacting with employees at various levels

• Results-oriented, proactive, and able to work independently in a dynamic environment

• Ability to manage multiple deadlines and priorities

• Knowledge of Mandarin is required to cover China coverage bankers

To apply for this position, please use the following URL:

https://ars2.equest.com/?response_id=f25419ef5d141e8b6c3f9063c091f1d7

postedCompany.description:
About J.P. Morgan

J.P. Morgan has been a leader in helping companies grow and markets develop throughout our history. We collaborate across the globe to deliver the solutions and advice to meet our clients’ needs, anywhere in the world.  We operate in 100 countries and hold global leadership positions across our businesses. When you join J.P. Morgan you’ll be: working with a team committed to being the best; earning the trust of our clients; demanding excellence of yourself.

What makes this the best place to build your career? Because we’ll make the most of all your talents, not just your technical skills. We’ll challenge you, but we’ll also make being here as stimulating and diverse as your life is. You’ll be given the tools, training and support needed to help us keep doing first-class business in a first-class way, as we have for over 200 years. Our global platform allows you to have more mobility, choices and opportunities to contribute than you ever imagined. It all adds up to the perfect opportunity to take your career further with us.
postedCompany.name: JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.
